task.title: Define the first typed event-log and replay slice
task.objective: Establish the smallest approved, durable kernel contract for typed append and deterministic replay.
acceptance_criteria: An accepted ADR records the envelope, stream/version semantics, SQLite durability boundary, and replay compatibility contract.
Behavior tests are written before implementation for ordered append/replay and stale-writer rejection.
A synchronous typed API persists one event per transaction and replays contiguous committed events after reopening.
Unknown event types or payload versions, malformed payloads, and stored version gaps fail visibly.
The complete pinned quality gate and required pull-request checks pass.

attempts.summary: Added the vela-kernel manifest and event-log behavior tests before the implementation existed.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused test target failed to compile because vela_kernel::event_log did not exist.
attempts.patch: Added the workspace member and initial restart replay, stale expected-version, missing-stream, and StreamId tests.

attempts.summary: Implemented the minimum SQLite append/replay API and expanded compatibility tests and documentation.
attempts.patch: Added crates/vela-kernel, ADR 0002, event-log behavior documentation, and tests for unsupported discriminators, malformed payloads, and sequence gaps.

attempts.summary: Applied independent pre-commit and CodeRabbit findings before merge.
attempts.diagnostic: Independent review identified an unnecessary SQLite 3.37 STRICT-table portability requirement; CodeRabbit found that Event::decode could fabricate log-level ReplayError variants and stream positions.
attempts.patch: Removed STRICT from the schema and introduced DecodeError so the log alone maps decoder failures to authoritative replay positions.

outcome.summary: Vela now has an approved local SQLite event log with atomic optimistic append, restart-safe ordered typed replay, and explicit compatibility failures.

verification.command: GitHub Rust quality gate for pull request 345

lessons: Approve persisted envelopes and durability boundaries before encoding them in implementation details.
Keep domain decoding errors narrower than storage and ordering errors so replay can attach authoritative persisted context.
Avoid optional database syntax that raises the minimum host-library version without adding value to the bounded contract.
An immediate SQLite transaction makes the expected-version check and single-row append one atomic concurrency boundary.
